tools.name: get_conditional_outcomes
tools.description: Measured HISTORY of forward returns after a stated condition — the base rate from this feed's own data, replacing folklore like "high funding = squeeze". Answers: "What happened historically after funding above the 90th percentile?", "When OBI stayed bid-heavy while funding was positive, what did the next 24h look like?". Condition fields AND together (state at least one). Funding (multi-year series): funding_pct_gte/lte (AS-OF percentile 0..100 — the rank the live endpoint showed at that moment, no look-ahead), streak_h_gte (hours funding kept its sign), funding_sign (positive|negative). Archive (v2, 15-min state archive — YOUNG series, growing daily): archive_feature ∈ obi_skew (−1..+1) | oi_chg_1h_pct (%) | taker_buy_ratio (0..1) | basis_bps, with archive_gte/lte (raw value, no sample floor) and archive_pct_gte/lte (as-of percentile). An archive condition shrinks the match window to the INTERSECTION of the series' spans — the response window and coverage.archive.{window_days,n_samples} disclose exactly how thin the evidence is; weight accordingly. horizons: ["4h","24h","72h"] default, each 1h..168h. Per horizon: n_matches (raw), n_effective (overlap-collapsed — overlapping forward windows are NOT independent samples; all stats use the collapsed set), median/p10/p90 forward return in PERCENT, hit_rate_up (RATIO 0..1), max_dd_median_pct (median worst drawdown before the horizon resolves, ≤0). verdict=history_silent means the condition matched too rarely to measure (n_effective<12 on every horizon): a first-class answer — you are trading on belief, size accordingly; stats are null, never noise. warnings flag overlap collapse, 14-day clustering and silenced horizons. matches_sample = up to 50 audit timestamps. Percentile-form conditions exist only once the ranked series has coverage.warmup_samples (12) points — a STRUCTURAL floor (a rank among 2 points is degenerate), not a maturity gate; nothing time-based is withheld. Prices: Binance 1h closes (coverage.price_src). REPORTS HISTORY, NOT PREDICTIONS — past conditional distributions do not imply future returns. Read-only.

archive_feature.description:
v2: condition on ONE archived state feature (15-min archive):
`obi_skew` (−1..+1), `oi_chg_1h_pct` (%), `taker_buy_ratio` (0..1),
`basis_bps`. ANDs with the funding fields; the match window becomes
the INTERSECTION of the constrained series' spans. Needs at least one
archive threshold below.

archive_gte.description:
Match hours where the archive feature's RAW value ≥ this (raw
thresholds have no sample floor — they exist from the first point).

archive_lte.description: Match hours where the archive feature's RAW value ≤ this.

archive_pct_gte.description:
Match hours where the feature's AS-OF percentile ≥ this (0..=100; the
rank exists only once its series has warmup_samples points).

archive_pct_lte.description: Match hours where the feature's as-of percentile ≤ this (0..=100).

funding_pct_gte.description:
Match hours where the AS-OF funding percentile was ≥ this (0..=100).
"As-of" = the rank the live endpoint reported at that moment — computed
only from data available then, no look-ahead.

funding_pct_lte.description: Match hours where the as-of funding percentile was ≤ this (0..=100).

funding_sign.description: Funding sign filter: `positive` (longs pay shorts) or `negative`.

streak_h_gte.description: Match hours where funding had kept its sign for at least this many hours.

symbol.description:
Asset symbol: BTC, ETH, SOL, BNB, XRP or DOGE (case-insensitive; a
`USDT` suffix is accepted).

FundingSign.description: Sign filter for the funding condition. `positive` = longs pay shorts.

HorizonOutcome.description:
Forward-return distribution at one horizon. Stats are `null` when
`n_effective` is below the measurability floor (12) — that horizon is
history-silent, NOT zero.

hit_rate_up.description: Share of effective matches with a positive forward return, 0..1.

max_dd_median_pct.description:
Median worst drawdown from entry close within (t, t+h], percent (≤ 0;
computed on hourly lows — the pain endured before the horizon resolves).

median_pct.description: Median forward return close(t+h)/close(t) − 1, in percent.

n_effective.description:
Matches thinned so consecutive kept matches are ≥ one horizon apart —
overlapping forward windows are NOT independent samples. Stats use these.

n_matches.description: Raw condition matches on the hourly grid (before overlap collapse).

p10_pct.description: 10th percentile of forward returns, percent (downside tail).

p90_pct.description: 90th percentile of forward returns, percent (upside tail).

OutcomesArchiveCoverage.description: Provenance of the archive-feature series (v2), when one is constrained.

window_days.description:
Span of the feature's archived series, days (young archives are SHORT —
weight accordingly; the answer is honest, not deep).

OutcomesCondition.description:
The condition being tested, echoed back as parsed. Any subset of fields may
be set (they AND together); at least one must be. Percentiles refer to the
AS-OF percentile: the rank the live endpoint would have reported at that
moment, computed only from data ≤ t (no look-ahead).

archive_feature.description:
Archive feature to condition on (v2, from the 15-min state archive):
`obi_skew` (−1..+1), `oi_chg_1h_pct` (%), `taker_buy_ratio` (0..1),
`basis_bps`. One per request; ANDs with the funding fields. The match
window becomes the INTERSECTION of the constrained series' spans.

archive_gte.description:
Match hours where the archive feature's RAW value ≥ this (no sample
floor — raw thresholds exist from the first archived point).

archive_lte.description: Match hours where the archive feature's RAW value ≤ this.

archive_pct_gte.description:
Match hours where the feature's AS-OF percentile ≥ this (0..100).
Rank exists only once its series has `warmup_samples` points.

archive_pct_lte.description: Match hours where the feature's as-of percentile ≤ this (0..100).

funding_pct_gte.description: Match hours where the as-of funding percentile ≥ this (0..100).

funding_pct_lte.description: Match hours where the as-of funding percentile ≤ this (0..100).

funding_sign.description: Match hours where the cross-venue funding rate has this sign.

streak_h_gte.description: Match hours where the same-sign funding streak is ≥ this many hours.

OutcomesCoverage.description: What fed the answer — features used, price source, structural floors.

archive.description: Provenance of the archive feature, `null` when none was constrained.

features.description: Condition features the request actually constrained.

price_src.description:
Price series used for forward returns (single-venue by design — no
consolidated/venue stitching inside one backtest series).

warmup_samples.description:
STRUCTURAL floor: an as-of percentile exists only once its series has
this many samples (a rank among 2 points is degenerate, not immature).
Raw thresholds have no floor. This is mechanics, not a maturity gate.

venues.description: Venues in the consistent-deep cross-venue set the series was built from.

OutcomesVerdict.description:
`measurable` = at least one requested horizon has n_effective ≥ 12.
`history_silent` = the condition matched too rarely to measure — a
first-class answer: you are trading on belief, size accordingly.

OutcomesWindow.description:
The history window the condition was evaluated over — the span of the
constrained series (their INTERSECTION when an archive feature joins the
funding series; dates are UTC `YYYY-MM-DD`).

condition.description: The condition as parsed — verify the server understood your hypothesis.

matches_sample.description:
Up to 50 raw match timestamps, oldest first, RFC3339 UTC — the audit
trail: any episode can be checked by hand.

warnings.description: Honest caveats: overlap collapse counts, clustering, silenced horizons.

tools.name: get_funding_percentile
tools.description: Current perpetual funding rate for a symbol, ranked vs its own multi-year history as a percentile (0-100). Answers: "Is BTC funding unusually high?", "What percentile is ETH funding at?". Returns rate_bps (basis points, 8h-normalized, cross-venue; positive = longs pay shorts), percentile (0-100), streak_h (hours the rate has kept its sign), and the coverage (venues + history depth backing the rank). Read-only.

symbol.description:
Asset symbol: BTC, ETH, SOL, BNB, XRP or DOGE (case-insensitive; a
`USDT` suffix is accepted, e.g. `BTCUSDT`).

CoverageEntry.description: Availability of a single metric group. `partial=false` + no `reason` means full data.

CoverageReason.description: Why a metric's `coverage` is `partial`. Absent/`null` when the metric is full.

oneOf.description: Compute exists (or will) but the trailing window is still filling.
Source or compute not deployed yet.
A venue in the set is unhealthy this cycle; metric on the reduced set.
The live source timestamp exceeds the freshness bound.

as_of.description: Assembly wall-time of the underlying state, RFC3339 UTC.

coverage.description: Availability of the funding metric (venues, window depth, reason).

percentile.description: Rank (0–100) of the current rate in its own trailing distribution.

rate_bps.description: Current cross-venue funding rate, 8h-normalized, in bps.

streak_h.description: Same-sign streak of the rate, in hours.

tools.name: get_liquidations_recent
tools.description: Recent cross-exchange liquidation totals for a symbol. Answers: "How much got liquidated on ETH in the last hour?", "Mostly longs or shorts?". Covers Binance, Bybit, OKX (not Hyperliquid). Returns whole-dollar totals, long_ratio (0..1 share of LONGS liquidated) and event count for the window (window_s, default 3600, max 86400). Read-only.

symbol.description:
Asset symbol: BTC, ETH, SOL, BNB, XRP or DOGE (case-insensitive; a
`USDT` suffix is accepted).

window_s.description:
Look-back window in seconds, 1..=86400 (default 3600). Values beyond
24 h are rejected, not clamped.

long_ratio.description:
Share of LONGS liquidated, RATIO 0..1 (0.71 = 71 % longs). `null` when
the window had zero notional.

n_events.description: Number of liquidation events in the window.

usd_total.description: Total liquidated notional in the window, USD (whole dollars).

window_s.description: The window actually used, seconds (upstream clamps to at most 86400).

tools.name: get_market_state
tools.description: Single normalized perpetual-futures microstructure snapshot for one crypto symbol, aggregated across Binance, Bybit, OKX and Hyperliquid. Answers: "What's the market state for BTC?", "Is ETH positioning stretched?". Covers BTC, ETH, SOL, BNB, XRP, DOGE. Returns funding+2y-percentile, OI, volume, CVD aggression, order-book imbalance, recent liquidation pressure, basis, and key drivers. Scales: *_ratio fields are 0..1, percentiles are 0-100, rel.* are multipliers of the trailing median (1.4 = 140%), obi.skew is -1..+1 (positive = bid-heavy), cvd.delta_usd positive = net taker buying, basis_bps positive = perp above spot index. Every metric carries a coverage entry (venues, window depth, partial reason) — weight confidence by it. Read-only, ~30s fresh. Use first for market context; for the historical base rate of a condition you observe here (e.g. funding percentile ≥ 90), follow with get_conditional_outcomes.

symbol.description:
Asset symbol: BTC, ETH, SOL, BNB, XRP or DOGE (case-insensitive; a
`USDT` suffix is accepted, e.g. `BTCUSDT`).

Calendar.description:
Regime-relevant clock: funding settlements + option expiry. These are the
scheduled events that break microstructure regimes.

funding_next.description:
Next funding settlement per venue (Binance live from the venue; others
rule-based: 8h at 00/08/16 UTC, Hyperliquid hourly).

next_option_expiry.description:
Next Deribit option expiry (daily 08:00 UTC; monthlies/quarterlies are
the last Friday — the large ones), RFC3339 UTC.

Coverage.description: Per-metric data-availability descriptor (hybrid model: clean values + this block).

CoverageEntry.description: Availability of a single metric group. `partial=false` + no `reason` means full data.

CoverageReason.description: Why a metric's `coverage` is `partial`. Absent/`null` when the metric is full.

oneOf.description: Compute exists (or will) but the trailing window is still filling.
Source or compute not deployed yet.
A venue in the set is unhealthy this cycle; metric on the reduced set.
The live source timestamp exceeds the freshness bound.

Cvd.description: Cumulative volume delta over a rolling window (`30m` or `2h`).

delta_usd.description:
Signed taker notional over the window, USD: POSITIVE = net taker
BUYING (buy − sell).

price_chg_pct.description: Price change over the same window, PERCENT (−0.8 = fell 0.8 %).

Depth.description: Order-book depth near the touch, from the four venues' own books.

ask_usd.description: Ask-side resting USD by distance band.

bid_usd.description: Bid-side resting USD by distance band.

spread_bps.description:
TIGHTEST single-venue spread, bps — executable on that venue, never
negative. (A consolidated cross-venue "spread" can cross and is not
tradeable; venue dislocation lives in `venues.*.mid_div_bps`.)

visibility.description: Where each side's visibility ends (both bounds in BPS from mid).

DepthBands.description:
Resting depth (USD notional) within N bps of the consensus mid, one side —
summed across the four venues' books. Bounded by each venue's visible
top-200 levels: a band BEYOND this side's `visibility.visible_to_bps` is a
LOWER BOUND, not a measurement — do not compare bid vs ask at a band
either side has outgrown (check both sides' `visible_to_bps` first).

10bps.description: Sum within ±10 bps of mid, USD.

25bps.description: Sum within ±25 bps of mid, USD.

50bps.description: Sum within ±50 bps of mid, USD.

DepthVisibility.description:
Per-side visibility of the summed books. Each venue serves its top-200
levels; dense books end nearer the mid than sparse ones, so between the
two bounds sums are FLOORS (some venues still contribute, some ended).

Feed.description:
Feed self-description — the staleness beacon. MCP clients cache tools/list
(names/descriptions/schemas) while response DATA flows live, so an agent
holding an old catalog still sees this block.

tools.description:
Number of tools this server currently exposes. If it exceeds the tools
your client offers, your connector's cached catalog is STALE — tell the
user to reconnect the connector to unlock the newer tools.

version.description: Published contract/registry version of the server answering you.

Funding.description: OI-informed cross-venue funding. `rate_bps` is the 8h-normalized rate ×1e4.

percentile.description: Rank (0-100) of the current rate in its own trailing distribution.

streak_h.description: Same-sign streak of the headline rate, in hours.

FundingNext.description: Per-venue next funding settlement times, RFC3339 UTC.

long_ratio.description: Share of LONGS liquidated, RATIO 0..1 (0.71 = 71 % longs).

Oi.description:
Open interest. `usd` is cross-venue notional; `rel` keys match the
`coverage."oi.rel"` entry.

chg_1h_pct.description:
% change vs 1 h ago on the SAME venue set (venue-consistent).
Sign vs price sign gives the 4-quadrant read: OI↑+price↑ = new longs,
OI↑+price↓ = new shorts, OI↓ = position closing.

PercentileWindows.description:
Percentile ranks (0–100) of the current value in its trailing windows;
keys match the `coverage."liq.percentile"` entry.

Price.description: Consolidated cross-exchange price context (equal-weight venue mids).

atr_1h_pct.description:
ATR(14) on 1 h bars BUILT FROM 1-MIN CONSOLIDATED MIDS (intraminute
wicks excluded — slightly understates true range; use `get_ohlcv` for
exact candles), as % of `last`.

chg_1h_pct.description: % change vs 1 h ago (consolidated 1-min mids; −0.8 = fell 0.8 %).

rv_24h_pct.description: Realized volatility: stdev of 5-min log returns over 24 h, ANNUALIZED, %.

RelWindows.description:
Rel-to-median multipliers per trailing window (`1.4` = 140 % of the
window's hourly median — a MULTIPLIER, not a percent).

1d.description: Current vs the trailing 1-day hourly median (multiplier, ×).

1m.description: Current vs the trailing 1-month hourly median (multiplier, ×).

1w.description: Current vs the trailing 1-week hourly median (multiplier, ×).

SideVisibility.description: One side's visibility bounds, bps from the consensus mid.

complete_to_bps.description:
Up to here EVERY fresh venue's book contributes → band sums at or
below this are complete totals, bps.

visible_to_bps.description:
Beyond here NO venue shows anything (the deepest visible level) →
band sums past this cannot grow: equal bands mean "ran out of book",
not equal liquidity, bps.

VenueSlice.description:
One venue's slice of the cross-exchange picture — divergence across venues
is itself a signal the aggregates deliberately smooth away.

cvd_30m_usd.description: 30-min CVD, USD (positive = net taker buying on this venue).

funding_bps.description: Latest funding rate, 8h-normalized, bps (positive = longs pay shorts).

mid_div_bps.description: This venue's mid vs the consolidated mid, bps (positive = trades rich).

Venues.description:
Per-venue slices (fields null where a venue lacks the metric — e.g. no
Hyperliquid entry in a Binance-only series).

age_seconds.description: Worst-case age (seconds) across the live sources that fed the non-null live fields.

basis_bps.description:
Perp-vs-spot basis in bps: POSITIVE = perp trades ABOVE the spot
index ((mark − index) / index × 1e4).

drivers.description: Fact-strings for fields that have data; no verdict.

feed.description:
Server self-description: compare `feed.tools` with the tools you can
call — fewer on your side means a stale connector catalog (reconnect).

stale_venues.description: Venues currently degraded / dropped from consensus this cycle.

tools.name: get_ohlcv
tools.description: Consolidated cross-exchange OHLCV candles for a symbol (all four venues' trades merged onto one timeline). Answers: "Show me BTC 1h candles", "What's the 24h range?". Use for stop placement, position sizing (ATR), R:R and realized-volatility math — the exact-candle complement to get_market_state's approximate price block. Intervals: 5m, 15m, 1h, 4h, 1d (default 1h); limit 1..=200 candles (default 100), oldest first. The NEWEST candle may be partial:true (bucket still forming) — exclude it from ATR/RV math. Fields: t = bucket open epoch ms, o/h/l/c = USD, v = traded volume in BASE units (BTC, ETH, ...), NOT USD — multiply by price for approximate notional. Read-only.

symbol.description:
Asset symbol: BTC, ETH, SOL, BNB, XRP or DOGE (case-insensitive; a
`USDT` suffix is accepted).

Candle.description:
One OHLCV candle (consolidated cross-exchange trades, all four venues on
one timeline).

partial.description:
`true` when the bucket is STILL FORMING (its close time is in the
future): o/h/l/c/v cover only part of the interval. EXCLUDE partial
candles from ATR/RV/average-volume math — a 1-minute-old hourly bar
otherwise crushes the last sample.

v.description: Traded volume in the bucket, BASE units (BTC, ETH, …) — NOT USD.

count.description: Number of candles returned (may be fewer than `limit` at history edges).

tools.name: get_state_history
tools.description: Time series of any numeric market-state fields from the 15-minute full-state archive — the derivative/trend view get_market_state cannot give. Answers: "How did OI build over the last 3 days?", "Is funding percentile rising or falling this week?", "When did the book skew flip?". fields = dotted numeric paths of get_market_state (1..=16), e.g. funding.percentile, oi.usd, obi.skew, cvd.delta_usd, basis_bps, price.last, depth.bid_usd.10bps, venues.binance.oi_usd. Returns parallel arrays: t (epoch ms, ascending) + series[field] aligned with t; null = that field was not populated in the archived row (honest hole, common near the archive's start — blocks accrue at their own pace). Downsampling: max_points 1..=1000 (default 200); stride in the response says how many 15-min rows each point skips; the newest row is never sampled away. coverage.archive_from/to are the WHOLE archive's bounds — the archive is YOUNG (born 2026-07-03) and grows a row per 15 min per asset; a thin answer is honest, not broken. from/to accept RFC3339, YYYY-MM-DD or epoch ms. Read-only.

fields.description:
Dotted numeric paths of the market state, 1..=16 per request — e.g.
`funding.percentile`, `oi.usd`, `obi.skew`, `cvd.delta_usd`,
`basis_bps`, `price.last`, `depth.bid_usd.10bps`,
`venues.binance.oi_usd`. Typos get an error naming the offender.

from.description:
Range start: RFC3339 (`2026-07-03T08:00:00Z`), `YYYY-MM-DD` (UTC
midnight), or epoch milliseconds. Default: the whole archive.

max_points.description:
Points returned after downsampling, 1..=1000 (default 200). The
response's `stride` says how many archived rows each point skips.

symbol.description:
Asset symbol: BTC, ETH, SOL, BNB, XRP or DOGE (case-insensitive; a
`USDT` suffix is accepted).

to.description: Range end, same formats. Default: the whole archive.

HistoryCoverage.description: Provenance of the archive behind a history answer.

archive_from.description:
First archived tick, RFC3339 UTC — `null` only while the archive is
completely empty.

step_minutes_nominal.description: Writer cadence between rows, minutes (15; holes possible).

from.description: First returned point, RFC3339 UTC (empty string when no points).

n_available.description: Rows available in the requested range BEFORE downsampling.

series.description: Requested dotted field → one value per point (aligned with `t`).

stride.description: Downsampling stride actually applied (1 = every archived row).

t.description: Epoch-ms per returned point, ascending (ms like `get_ohlcv`'s `t`).

to.description: Last returned point, RFC3339 UTC (empty string when no points).
